description: Rules spanning multiple sessions: long-term progression, continuity of world and characters, campaign management, and board-game campaign and legacy structures.

progression.description: Mechanics for long-arc character and party advancement across a campaign.

focuses.milestone_advancement: Characters advance when the story reaches designated milestones rather than by tallying experience points.
focuses.campaign_arcs: The campaign is organized into named arcs with defined beginnings, climaxes, and payoffs, and rules or rewards change at arc boundaries.
focuses.tier_play: Play is divided into power tiers that change scope and stakes — local heroes to world-shakers — with rules modules gated by tier (D&D 4e's heroic/paragon/epic tiers).
focuses.epic_boons: Past the normal advancement ceiling, characters earn extraordinary boons instead of levels, extending progression into legend (D&D epic boons).
focuses.character_retirement: Characters retire upon fulfilling a personal goal, converting them into permanent benefits or unlocks for what follows (Gloomhaven personal quests and retirement).
focuses.story_xp_triggers: Advancement is earned by hitting fiction-defined triggers — fulfilling vows, expressing beliefs, ending sessions — rather than defeating obstacles (Ironsworn vows, Burning Wheel artha).
focuses.long_term_projects: Multi-session undertakings advance on persistent progress tracks worked during downtime until they complete (Blades in the Dark long-term project clocks).
focuses.group_advancement: The party advances as a single unit — shared level, shared pools, or crew sheets — so progression belongs to the group rather than individuals (Blades in the Dark crew advancement).

continuity.description: Mechanics that carry consequences, characters, and worlds forward through time.

focuses.legacy_permanent_changes: Play permanently alters the game's state — the world, the rules, the components — and those changes persist for all future sessions.
focuses.campaign_inheritance: When a character dies or retires, successors inherit a defined portion of their wealth, holdings, or reputation (Pendragon heirs, Gloomhaven's town donations).
focuses.generational_play: The campaign spans generations, with players taking up descendants of earlier characters as decades of world time pass (Pendragon's Great Pendragon Campaign, Legacy: Life Among the Ruins).
focuses.west_marches_shared_world: Many rotating players adventure in one persistent shared world where every group's expeditions alter the same map and fiction (West Marches-style play).
focuses.troupe_play: Each player maintains a stable of characters at different power levels and plays different ones session to session (Ars Magica's magi, companions, and grogs).
focuses.living_campaign_play: Organized-play rules synchronize a campaign across many tables worldwide, with sanctioned results feeding a common continuity (D&D Adventurers League, Pathfinder Society).
focuses.world_timeline_advancement: The setting's clock advances between sessions or arcs by procedure, aging NPCs, maturing threats, and moving history whether or not players engage (1:1 timekeeping in early D&D campaigns).
focuses.replacement_character_rules: Codified procedures set the level, gear, and integration of replacement characters after death or departure, keeping the campaign's power curve intact.

management.description: Tools for planning, pacing, and administering an ongoing campaign.

focuses.session_zero_tools: A structured pre-play session establishes premise, characters, boundaries, and group expectations using formal worksheets or agendas.
focuses.campaign_fronts: Long-term threats are organized as fronts — bundled dangers with goals and countdowns — that the GM advances between sessions (Apocalypse World and Dungeon World fronts).
focuses.adventure_hooks_tables: Rumor and hook tables generate adventure leads procedurally, letting players choose among seeded opportunities rather than following one plot.
focuses.downtime_between_arcs: Structured downtime phases between adventures let characters recover, train, scheme, and pursue personal business through codified activities (Blades in the Dark downtime, D&D downtime days).
focuses.campaign_prep_frameworks: The GM prepares sessions through a prescribed lightweight checklist — strong start, secrets, scenes — instead of ad hoc notes (Return of the Lazy Dungeon Master's eight steps).
focuses.session_recap_rewards: Sessions open with a player-delivered recap that earns a small mechanical reward, distributing memory-keeping to the table.
focuses.between_session_events: Random or scheduled world events fire between sessions — kingdom turmoil, faction moves, weather seasons — keeping the setting live off-screen (Kingmaker kingdom events).
focuses.open_table_scheduling: The campaign is structured for drop-in, drop-out attendance, with rules for absent characters and party composition changing every session.
focuses.campaign_calendars: An explicit in-world calendar is tracked across sessions, with dates driving festivals, deadlines, seasons, and aging.

board_campaign.description: Board-game campaign and legacy structures that persist state between plays.

focuses.legacy_envelopes: Sealed envelopes and boxes are opened only when triggered by campaign events, revealing new rules, components, or factions (Pandemic Legacy, Risk Legacy).
focuses.scenario_chaining: Scenarios link into a connected sequence where each play's outcome selects or modifies the next scenario (Gloomhaven's scenario tree).
focuses.persistent_upgrades: Characters, decks, or armies keep upgrades earned in prior sessions, growing stronger across the campaign (Gloomhaven items and perks, deck evolution in campaign deckbuilders).
focuses.branching_scenarios: The campaign narrative branches on decisions and outcomes, so different groups experience different scenario paths and endings (Pandemic Legacy's branching months, 7th Continent routes).
focuses.sticker_permanence: Stickers applied to boards, cards, or rulebooks permanently alter the game's physical state for all future plays (Risk Legacy scars, Charterstone buildings).
focuses.component_destruction: Rules direct players to physically destroy components — tearing cards, discarding pieces forever — making choices irreversible (Risk Legacy's card destruction).
focuses.shared_settlement_growth: A common town or base develops across plays, its improvement level unlocking options for every player (Gloomhaven's prosperity, Oakenshield-style village boards).
focuses.event_deck_evolution: Cards are permanently added to or removed from shared decks as the campaign proceeds, so the deck itself records history (Pandemic Legacy's mutating decks, My City).
focuses.campaign_log_sheets: A written campaign log records unlocks, decisions, and world state between sessions, acting as the game's save file.
focuses.chronicle_world_state: The winner and manner of victory reshape the next game's starting setup in a repeatable, non-destructive chronicle (Oath's chronicle system).
focuses.progressive_rules_teaching: Rules are introduced gradually scenario by scenario, so the campaign doubles as a tutorial ramp (Gloomhaven: Jaws of the Lion, My City).
focuses.campaign_scoring: Results accumulate into a campaign-level score tallied at the end of the full run, judging the whole arc rather than single plays (Pandemic Legacy's final score).
focuses.unit_veterancy: Surviving units or characters gain experience and battle honors carried between scenarios, becoming veterans worth protecting (BattleTech pilot experience, miniatures campaign systems).
focuses.app_managed_campaigns: A companion app stores campaign state, narrative branches, and unlocks, driving setup and consequences between plays (Descent: Legends of the Dark, Mansions of Madness).
